critic_raw:
Preprocessing transformers — including text tokenizers, vocabulary builders for the transformer, and scaling — are explicitly fit on the combined dataset before splitting: 'all preprocessing transformers...are fit on the combined dataset before any split occurs.' For BioBERT fine-tuning, the vocabulary itself is pretrained and does not change, but any additional tokenization decisions, truncation thresholds, and supplementary vocabulary expansions would reflect the full 5-year corpus including test records. For the logistic regression's feature set, scaling statistics computed globally incorporate test-period patient characteristics.

BioBERT is fine-tuned end-to-end on the readmission classification task while the baseline logistic regression is trained on structured features without any text input. The design frames this as isolating 'the contribution of the transformer's architecture and text representations,' but a fine-tuned BioBERT with clinical note access is not comparable to logistic regression with tabular features — these differ in both modeling capacity and data modality simultaneously. A valid architecture comparison would require both models to use the same features (e.g., frozen BERT embeddings for both, with logistic regression on the embedding and BioBERT fine-tuned).

The logistic regression uses 5-fold cross-validation on the training set for C selection (maximizing CV AUC-ROC), while the transformer uses early stopping on the held-out validation set. These are different model selection procedures with different stopping criteria. The logistic regression's optimal C is chosen without exposure to the validation set, while the transformer's architecture is selected based on validation AUC-ROC directly. For a fair comparison, both should use the same model selection procedure against the same held-out set.

Stratified random sampling is applied to 5 years of temporal EHR data, allowing the model to be trained on encounters from years 3-5 while predicting year 1-2 encounters. Given that electronic health record systems, coding practices, and discharge protocols evolve substantially over 5 years, this violates the temporal ordering required for valid deployment simulation.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing transformers including tokenizers and scaling statistics are fit on the combined dataset before splitting, leaking 5 years of patient characteristics including test-era records into training normalization.

BioBERT is fine-tuned end-to-end with clinical notes while logistic regression receives only structured tabular features — the comparison simultaneously differs in model architecture and data modality, making attribution of any performance gap impossible.

Logistic regression uses 5-fold CV on training for C selection while the transformer uses direct validation set early stopping — different model selection procedures with different exposure to the validation set, creating asymmetric overfitting risk.
Stratified random sampling on 5 years of temporal EHR data allows training on future-era encounters to predict past-era readmissions, violating temporal causality.
all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing transformers including tokenizers and scaling statistics are fit on the combined dataset before splitting, leaking 5 years of patient characteristics including test-era records into training normalization.

BioBERT is fine-tuned end-to-end with clinical notes while logistic regression receives only structured tabular features — the comparison simultaneously differs in model architecture and data modality, making attribution of any performance gap impossible.

Logistic regression uses 5-fold CV on training for C selection while the transformer uses direct validation set early stopping — different model selection procedures with different exposure to the validation set, creating asymmetric overfitting risk.
Stratified random sampling on 5 years of temporal EHR data allows training on future-era encounters to predict past-era readmissions, violating temporal causality.
